metadata.notes: agent_supports: yes (/compress + auto-compression); daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready â€” compression is in-place (setHistory + updateMessagesFromHistory re-syncs the SAME JSONL, no new-file boundary), so the visible arc rides the normal user_messageâ†’turn_done path.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario asserts only the visible state arc around context compaction: `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` at the compaction boundary, no flicker to `waiting` mid-compaction, then a second normal turn (`ready â†’ working â†’ ready`) to prove the session continues. Each half maps to an event the gemini-cli adapter produces today.

### agent_supports: yes

Gemini CLI ships context compaction in two forms, confirmed in the installed v0.46.0 bundle (`@google/gemini-cli/bundle`, the JS equivalent of `strings`):

1. **User-initiated** â€” a `/compress` slash command, described in the bundle as `"Compresses the context by replacing it with a summary"` (slash-command def `name: "compress"`). NOTE: gemini's command is `/compress`, NOT Claude's `/compact` â€” the recipe uses `/compress`.
2. **Automatic** â€” `tryCompressChat(prompt_id, force, abortSignal)` fires against a `compressionThreshold` / `COMPRESSION_TOKEN_THRESHOLD`; the bundle help reads `"core automatically compresses the conversation history before sending"`. Either trigger drives the same state arc; only the trigger differs (the simpler recipe is `/compress`-driven).

The agent runs the summarization as a real LLM round-trip, so it genuinely transitions working during compaction. agent_supports = yes.

### daemon_capability: full

Source = `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats, Parser: JSONLineParser{Parser}}` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/geminicli/agent.go`). Walking the user-observable signals against `parser.go` as it stands:

1. **`ready â†’ working` at compaction start, `working` throughout, `working â†’ ready` when done.** Crucially, gemini compaction is IN-PLACE: it rewrites the in-memory chat via `getChat().setHistory(newHistory)`, then `chatRecordingService.updateMessagesFromHistory(history)` re-syncs the SAME persisted session JSONL. It does NOT open a new transcript file at the boundary â€” the opposite of newer Claude Code (2.1.x+), so the new-file/session-stitching risk that dominates the claudecode/2.12 caveats does NOT apply here. The summarizer's LLM call is itself recorded as a `gemini` assistant message; while it streams (empty/in-place placeholder, or a tool/no-final-text shape) `IsAgentDone()` stays false and the session holds `working`; when the summary lands as a final text-only `gemini` message with no open tool calls, `parseAssistant` reclassifies it `turn_done` (parser.go ~L209) and the session settles to `ready`. The `/compress` invocation arrives as a `user`-role text message â†’ `parseUser` emits `user_message` (readyâ†’working). That yields the asserted `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` for the compaction half.
2. **No flicker to `waiting` mid-compaction.** The parser never emits a waiting/permission signal for compression; `waiting` only arises from explicit approval prompts, which `gemini -y` (yolo) suppresses. Rule-4 default holds `working` until the inferred `turn_done`.
3. **The compaction event itself is opaque (correct, not a gap).** Gemini records compaction as a UI item `type: "compression"` (MessageType.COMPRESSION) with `originalTokenCount`/`newTokenCount`. The parser's `parseMessage` dispatches only `type:"user"` and `type:"gemini"`; everything else â€” including `"compression"` â€” falls to `default: return false` (skip, commented `// system / info / compression / unknown`). So irrlicht treats compaction as an opaque working-episode, exactly as the claudecode cell does (irrlicht's `CompactionState` domain field is dead code with no production writer). The spec only asserts the state arc, so this is full, not a gap.
4. **Post-compaction normal turn** rides the standard `user_message` â†’ final text-only `turn_done` path â†’ `ready â†’ working â†’ ready`.

Both spec-required arcs are emitted by the parser today, so the daemon is `full`. The cwd-only-in-body wrinkle (cwd encoded inside the bootstrap `<session_context>`, harvested onto every event) affects PID binding, not the lifecycle arc.

### driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (`replaydata/agents/gemini-cli/driver-interactive.sh`) launches `gemini -y` under tmux and genuinely elicits `send slash wait_turn sleep` (its `DRIVE_ELICITS` constant). The recipe needs exactly those four: `send` (turn-1 prompt + turn-2 prompt), `slash` (`/compress` â€” an inline-argument-less slash command, identical keystrokes to `send`, so a `slash` step, NOT a `keys` gap), `wait_turn` (block on the inferred text-only `turn_done`), and `sleep` (let the daemon observe each settle + give the in-place compaction time). No stubbed primitive (interrupt/keys/reset_session/restart/resume/sigkill/exit_clean/start_session/session) is exercised. driver = ready. An INTERACTIVE recipe is used deliberately: a headless `gemini -p` run exits at turn completion before the daemon can observe the terminal ready edge, and headless mode cannot deliver an in-REPL `/compress` anyway.

## Why confidence 0.7, not higher

The verdict is grounded in the bundle scan (feature shipped), the parser read (arc emitted), and the real testdata transcript (`$set` + `user`/`gemini` shapes confirmed). But two properties are emission-dependent and a recording is the only thing that can promote them from provisional to settled: (a) that the summarizer's compaction call actually lands as a settle-able final text-only `gemini` message (vs. a shape that leaves the turn open), and (b) that `updateMessagesFromHistory`'s post-compaction `$set` rewrite is a no-op to the parser (the `$set` branch only harvests cwd and never opens/closes a turn â€” confirmed by reading `harvestSet`, but unconfirmed against a live compaction `$set`). These keep daemon=full but cap confidence in the honest multi-source band.
caveats: Gemini's command is `/compress` (NOT Claude's `/compact`); the bundle describes it as 'Compresses the context by replacing it with a summary'. Auto-compression (tryCompressChat / compressionThreshold) drives the same arc.

Provisional on emission: daemon=full is grounded in the parser read + bundle scan, but whether the compaction summarizer call lands as a settle-able final text-only 'gemini' message (turn_done) is emission-dependent â€” record promotes it from provisional to settled.

Compaction is IN-PLACE for gemini (setHistory + updateMessagesFromHistory re-sync the SAME JSONL via a $set rewrite) â€” NO new transcript file at the boundary, unlike newer Claude Code. The session-stitching / new-file risk that dominates the claudecode/2.12 caveats does not apply here; the post-compaction $set is a parser no-op (harvestSet only reads cwd).

The compaction event itself is opaque: gemini records it as a UI item type 'compression' (MessageType.COMPRESSION), which the parser's default branch skips. irrlicht cannot distinguish compaction from a normal long working-turn â€” same as the claudecode cell; the spec only asserts the visible state arc.

cwd lives only in the transcript body (<session_context>), not the path/process; PID binding relies on the parser's cwd harvest + EnrichNewSession LastCWD fallback. Session identity, not the lifecycle arc.

sources.note: v0.46.0 bundle scan (JS equivalent of `strings`): slash-command def name:"compress" with description 'Compresses the context by replacing it with a summary'; auto-compression via tryCompressChat(prompt_id,force,abortSignal) + compressionThreshold / COMPRESSION_TOKEN_THRESHOLD + 'core automatically compresses the conversation history before sending'. Compaction rewrites in-memory history (getChat().setHistory(newHistory)) then chatRecordingService.updateMessagesFromHistory(history) re-syncs the same JSONL; recorded as a UI item type:"compression" (MessageType.COMPRESSION) with originalTokenCount/newTokenCount.

parseUser â†’ user_message (readyâ†’working) for the /compress text message; parseAssistant infers turn_done from a final text-only 'gemini' message with no open tool calls (~L209, workingâ†’ready); parseMessage default branch skips type 'compression'/system/unknown (// system / info / compression / unknown, ~L107); harvestSet on a $set envelope only harvests cwd (post-compaction history rewrite is a parser no-op).

Source = FilesUnderRoot{~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats, JSONLineParser}; CommandPattern bin/gemini + isHeapBumpWorker ExcludeArgv; DiscoverPID binds the launcher by cwd â€” same in-place transcript the daemon tails across compaction.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"; gemini -y under tmux; wait_turn mirrors the parser's text-only/no-tool turn_done inference. The recipe needs only send/slash(/compress)/wait_turn/sleep â€” every step is genuinely elicited, no stubbed primitive.

Real gemini transcript: line 1 header (no type/$set), $set envelopes carrying messages (bootstrap) + lastUpdated heartbeats, bare user (content array) / gemini (content string, streamed in-place under one id) messages â€” grounds how the post-compaction history rewrite ($set) and the summarizer 'gemini' message appear on disk.

preconditions: gemini CLI >=0.45.2 on PATH and authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth-personal, no API key)
irrlichd is recording (dev daemon with --record, isolated IRRLICHT_ONBOARD_HOME + alt port)
setup: Driver launches `gemini -y` under tmux in a fresh cwd; gemini mints its own sessionId (harvested from the transcript header); first-run trust-folder prompt auto-accepted

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: still-here

verify: events.jsonl shows the session entering ready after launch
events.jsonl shows readyâ†’workingâ†’ready for the first turn
after /compress runs, the session settles back to ready (no stuck working, no waiting)
events.jsonl shows readyâ†’workingâ†’ready for the post-compaction turn
transcript.jsonl is the SAME file across the boundary (in-place compaction, no new session-*.jsonl)
